mnemonics: Most Number are in the Middle
keyPoints: 8.1: The first practical programming language, Plankalkül, by Konrad Zuse, was developed in 1946.
8.2: The first general-purpose electronic computer, ENIAC, was developed in 1946 by John Mauchly and J. Presper Eckert.
8.3: The first high-level language, Plankalkül, was developed in 1946 by Konrad Zuse.
8.4: The first compiler was developed in 1950 by John Backus, for the IBM 701 computer.
8.5: The first 8-bit microprocessor, the Intel 4004, was developed in 1971 by Ted Hoff and Stanley Mazor.
